blockingIssues.description: The dedicated pointer-receiver regression does not actually use a non-addressable receiver call site, so VAL-CODEGEN-015 is not truly covered.
The feature commit includes impl/Vec/HashMap/go-cleanliness/go-version coverage that belongs to a separate phase-7 feature, breaking feature-boundary contract clarity and reviewability.
The regression test only checks compile/build cleanliness and never runs the repaired example, so it does not prove end-to-end runtime behavior for the example pipeline.

The Go 1.26+ validation only checks the success path in the current environment and does not exercise the required user-visible warning/failure path for unsupported Go versions, leaving VAL-CROSS-007 unverified.
The typechecker change makes in-scope generic type parameters act as universal wildcards in `types_compatible`, introducing a soundness regression beyond the intended Self-substitution fix.
Codegen still drops type arguments when emitting zero values for generic nominal types, so error paths like `Result<Pair[A, B], str>` can still generate invalid Go.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce that workers must not pre-consume later feature scope even when the code already supports it; each feature should stay within its declared fulfills and verification boundary.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `phase-7-full-codegen-core-types-and-control-flow` found impl/collections/go-cleanliness coverage added in a commit whose feature description only covered core codegen responsibilities, while those responsibilities were assigned to `phase-7-impls-collections-and-go-cleanliness`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how to handle large rewrites where fully test-first sequencing is impractical, and distinguish semantic-only follow-up fixes from true codegen milestones when requiring manual `.rg -> .go` checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two reviews surfaced skill friction: the core Phase 7 rewrite explicitly deviated from test-first because the module was rewritten wholesale, and the generic Self semantic fix recorded a manual codegen check even though a separate later feature was still needed to repair generic Go emission.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library note that `go vet` is part of standard Phase 7 generated-Go cleanliness validation.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in mission AGENTS.md and reflected operationally in tests; no additional library entry was necessary.

rejectedObservations.observation: Record the feature-name mismatch between `phase-7-fix-result-option-example-fixture` and `phase-7-fix-result-option-example-regression` as shared-state knowledge.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous traceability issue for orchestrator cleanup rather than durable repository knowledge.
